etymology.description: 洗(洗) (xǐ, "wash") and 澡(澡) (zǎo, "bathe") combine to form a word meaning to take a bath or wash oneself. 洗(洗) (xǐ, "wash") indicates the act of cleaning, while 澡(澡) (zǎo, "bathe") specifically refers to the act of bathing. Together, they create a term for the complete process of cleansing the body through bathing.
etymology.interpretation: 洗(洗) (xǐ, "wash") and 澡(澡) (zǎo, "bathe") together describe the action of washing oneself in water, indicating the practice of personal hygiene.

examples.chinese: 她每天洗澡(她每天洗澡)。
examples.english: She has a bath every day.

examples.chinese: 我想在晚饭后洗澡(我想在晚飯後洗澡)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng zài wǎnfàn hòu xǐzǎo.
examples.english: I want to have a bath after dinner.

examples.chinese: 孩子不愿意洗澡(孩子不願意洗澡)，总是找借口(總是找藉口)。
examples.pinyin: Háizi bù yuànyì xǐzǎo, zǒng shì zhǎo jièkǒu.
examples.english: The child doesn't want to have a bath and always makes excuses.
